page: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or GPUs are bundled with this instance type.

Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and small-block read-only memory bandwidth. Redis SET operations and prompt processing for small LLM models also rank in the top tier. Single-core CPU performance, large-block memory bandwidth, and cryptographic operations fall into the strong tier. However, single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression show average performance.

The instance provides a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps but lacks complimentary public IPv4 addresses and local NVMe storage, requiring external network-attached storage. Given its high-density DDR5 memory and dedicated AMD EPYC cores, the instance offers qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring high multi-core throughput without the overhead of hyperthreading. It is suited for software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. In benchmark testing, the m7a.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution, software compilation, and Redis SET operations, alongside strong single-core and cryptographic capabilities. Conversely, single-threaded gzip compression and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression perform at average levels. Lacking local NVMe storage, this instance is suited for memory-intensive multi-core workloads, database operations, static web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated 64-core AMD EPYC instance with DDR5 memory, delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance and high-speed memory bandwidth for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
256 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory with 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU execution and software compilation
Strong capabilities in database operations and medium-scale LLM inference
No local storage or bundled GPUs, requiring external storage solutions
